name: Kuilima Cove
tagline: A tiny protected cove at Turtle Bay with beginner-friendly snorkeling and green sea turtles just offshore.
parking: Use the Turtle Bay Resort public beach access parking; free but can fill on weekends
bestTime: Morning for clearest water and turtle sightings; summer for calmest conditions
talkStory: Kuilima means 'to hold hands' or 'joining of arms' in Hawaiian, describing the protective rocky arms that shelter this tiny cove. The cove's calm, reef-protected water stays swimmable even when the rest of the North Shore is closed out by 20-foot winter surf. Green sea turtles are near-guaranteed sightings here; they feed on the algae growing on the rocks. It is a public beach despite being adjacent to Turtle Bay Resort — park in the public access lot and walk right in.
